what_it_does: Defines a tentative seed plan for redesigning the graph layout, documenting source references, data-to-screen mappings, visual rules, motion behavior, card behavior, verification needs, and open questions.
observations: Frames the document as a challengeable B artifact rather than a settled specification.
Lists sources of truth spanning directives, session history, recall, DNA files, composition files, substrate data, and current graph code.
Maps substrate dimensions such as depth, order, type, magnitude, edge direction, edge kind, and time onto proposed screen dimensions.
Proposes replacing radial layout and variable node radius with an even chipboard-style grid using constant node size.
Defines magnitude as folder glyph opacity with a constant ring opacity.
Seeds a type-to-colour map using muted palette roles and reserves gold or ochre for emphasis rather than resting type colour.
Specifies always-visible directed traces with calm bundled flow, arrowheads, and reduced-motion consideration.
Requires drill navigation to move continuously through persistent nodes and FLIP-style transitions rather than wholesale canvas replacement.
Keeps the tap card concept and identifies additional projection data to fold into it.
Lists tests, composability, addressing, and resolution expectations for the redesign.
Ends with open questions about time, colour ownership, edge colour, motion model, file surfacing, root node treatment, location, and cross-repo seams.
standouts: The data-to-screen mapping table is the central organizing device for the redesign.
The plan explicitly separates proposed decisions, assumptions, and open questions.
Motion continuity is treated as a core behavioral law rather than a visual enhancement.
The type-colour system is framed as a controlled semantic carve-out within a restrained design vocabulary.
conventions: Uses numbered sections and requirement references such as R1-R16.
Marks unsettled claims with [assumption], [open], and [debt] labels.
Keeps implementation references as file paths and line hints rather than embedding code.
Uses tables for mapping substrate dimensions and type colours.
Distinguishes visual projection rules from substrate data and from UI implementation notes.
concerns: The file mixes design intent, implementation direction, verification criteria, and backlog items in one document.
Several decisions remain open while nearby text uses directive language, which may require later normalization.
Some references are abbreviated or contextual, such as the transcript path using /.../.
The time dimension is acknowledged but left without a concrete screen mapping.
notes: Briefing note: this is a challenge-ready redesign plan for projecting substrate graph data into an on-DNA chipboard-style graph view.
questions: How should mtime appear in the visual projection?
Where should the type-to-colour map live as addressed data?
Should edge colour come from source type, target type, or edge kind?
Should drill motion use per-level FLIP transitions or a persistent zoomable scene?
How should file contents be surfaced without creating visual noise?
Should the root node remain visible, and if so where?
Where should the graph instrument live in the project structure?
How should the cross-repo composition seam be represented?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it presents a tentative, challengeable seed plan with assumptions, references, proposed redesign rules, and unresolved questions.
intention_signals: The file seems to be trying to turn a rejected or incomplete graph layout into a reviewable implementation plan that preserves traceability, exposes assumptions, and gives future agents concrete redesign targets.
novelty: It treats a graph redesign as a substrate projection ledger, tying visual dimensions, DNA constraints, motion law, test criteria, and unresolved questions into one challenge document.
contribution: Contributes a neutral redesign ledger for the graph view: source references, projection mappings, layout rules, semantic colour seeds, motion requirements, card integration notes, verification expectations, and explicit unresolved decisions for later fusion.
summary_for_embedding: A tentative graph redesign seed plan mapping substrate graph data to an on-DNA chipboard-style UI with grid layout, opacity-based magnitude, semantic type colours, visible directed traces, continuous drill motion, card behavior, tests, addressing, and open questions.
intention_for_embedding: This was written to give challengers and implementers a traceable, assumption-marked plan for replacing the existing graph layout with a responsive data-projection design.
